original_option: GOALS-BASED INVESTING allocates client wealth into SEPARATE PORTFOLIOS or SUB-PORTFOLIOS matched to specific goals: (1) essential needs (safety); (2) lifestyle goals (moderate risk); (3) aspirational goals (higher risk); each goal has its own return objective, risk tolerance, and time horizon; alternative to single-portfolio MVO approach; framework helps clients EMOTIONALLY understand risk-taking by goal.
new_option: GOALS-BASED INVESTING allocates client wealth into SEPARATE PORTFOLIOS or SUB-PORTFOLIOS matched to specific goals: (1) essential needs (safety).

original_option: ASSET LOCATION assigns different asset types to different ACCOUNT TYPES for tax efficiency: TAX-INEFFICIENT assets (high-turnover strategies, taxable bonds, REITs producing ordinary income) in TAX-DEFERRED accounts (401k, IRA); TAX-EFFICIENT assets (broad-market equity ETFs, municipal bonds, buy-and-hold equity) in TAXABLE accounts; ROTH accounts hold HIGHEST-GROWTH assets to maximize tax-free compounding; can add materially to after-tax returns.
new_option: ASSET LOCATION assigns different asset types to different ACCOUNT TYPES for tax efficiency: TAX-INEFFICIENT assets (high-turnover strategies, taxable bonds, REITs producing ordinary income) in TAX-DEFERRED accounts (401k, IRA).

original_option: TEY = municipal bond yield / (1 − marginal tax rate); allows comparison of tax-exempt muni yields to taxable bond yields on equivalent basis; e.g., muni yielding 3% for investor at 35% marginal rate: TEY = 3% / (1 − 0.35) = 3% / 0.65 = 4.62%; compare to 10-yr Treasury or corporate at similar credit/duration to determine muni attractiveness.
new_option: TEY = municipal bond yield / (1 − marginal tax rate).

original_option: AFTER-TAX RETURN = PRE-TAX RETURN × (1 − effective tax rate on that return type); for INCOME (dividends, interest): after-tax = pre-tax × (1 − ordinary rate); for CAPITAL GAINS (long-term): after-tax = pre-tax × (1 − LT capital gains rate); MIXED portfolio: weight by return components; ADD-BACK for tax-exempt income; SUBTRACT for taxes on distributions/turnover.
new_option: AFTER-TAX RETURN = PRE-TAX RETURN × (1 − effective tax rate on that return type).

original_option: 6.10% — dividends after-tax: 2% × (1 − 0.35) = 1.30%; capital gains after-tax: 6% × (1 − 0.20) = 4.80%; total after-tax return: 1.30% + 4.80% = 6.10%.

APPROXIMATION: Real ≈ Nominal − Inflation (adequate for small numbers); EXACT (Fisher): (1 + Real) = (1 + Nominal) / (1 + Inflation), so Real = (1 + Nominal)/(1 + Inflation) − 1; e.g., 8% nominal, 3% inflation: approximation 5.0%; exact (1.08/1.03) − 1 = 4.85%; approximation understates the difference and materially matters at high inflation.

with multiple ACCOUNT TYPES (taxable, tax-deferred, Roth), rebalancing must consider: (1) NO capital gains tax impact from selling in tax-deferred accounts (favor rebalancing there); (2) LARGE gain impact in taxable accounts (harvest LOSSES to offset); (3) different asset LOCATIONS may skew rebalancing direction; (4) Roth accounts have tax-free growth; strategic multi-account rebalancing can add basis points annually to after-tax returns.
new_option: with multiple ACCOUNT TYPES (taxable, tax-deferred, Roth), rebalancing must consider: (1) NO capital gains tax impact from selling in tax-deferred accounts (favor rebalancing there).

original_option: reporting should include: (1) TOTAL PORTFOLIO value and change; (2) PERFORMANCE by portfolio segment and vs BENCHMARKS; (3) ATTRIBUTION (asset allocation vs security selection); (4) AFTER-TAX and AFTER-FEE returns; (5) GOAL PROGRESS toward stated objectives; (6) RISK metrics (drawdown, vol); (7) REAL return net of inflation; (8) COST REPORTING (fees, taxes); reporting FREQUENCY typically quarterly with annual review.
new_option: reporting should include: (1) TOTAL PORTFOLIO value and change.

original_option: adviser plays a critical BEHAVIORAL COACH role: prevent PANIC-SELLING during drawdowns; discourage PERFORMANCE CHASING after strong runs; educate on LONG-TERM investment horizons; frame short-term volatility in context; adherence to IPS through market cycles is estimated by industry research to add 100-300 bps per year to realized returns vs abandonment of strategy; behavioral coaching is often the ADVISER'S HIGHEST VALUE-ADD.
new_option: adviser plays a critical BEHAVIORAL COACH role: prevent PANIC-SELLING during drawdowns.

original_option: adviser fees should be TRANSPARENT to the client: TYPES include (1) AUM-based fee (e.g., 1% of assets, declining at higher levels); (2) FLAT/RETAINER fee (fixed dollar amount); (3) HOURLY fee for planning; (4) COMMISSION-based (from product sales, subject to conflict of interest); FEE-ONLY typically preferred to eliminate product conflicts; disclose ALL fees including underlying fund expenses, custodial fees, transaction costs.
new_option: adviser fees should be TRANSPARENT to the client: TYPES include (1) AUM-based fee (e.g., 1% of assets, declining at higher levels).

original_option: for clients with CONCENTRATED HOLDINGS (large employer stock, founder position, inherited stake): DIVERSIFICATION strategies include: (1) OUTRIGHT SALE (subject to tax cost); (2) STAGED liquidation over years; (3) EXCHANGE FUNDS (pool with other concentrated holders); (4) COLLARS or PROTECTIVE PUTS to hedge; (5) CHARITABLE giving of appreciated shares (tax-efficient); (6) BORROWING against holdings; approach depends on tax, control, liquidity constraints; tax cost of diversification must be weighed against concentration risk.
new_option: for clients with CONCENTRATED HOLDINGS (large employer stock, founder position, inherited stake): DIVERSIFICATION strategies include: (1) OUTRIGHT SALE (subject to tax cost).

original_option: portfolio construction INTEGRATES with estate planning: (1) STEP-UP in basis on death for taxable assets — hold most-appreciated assets in taxable for step-up; (2) BENEFICIARY designations on tax-deferred accounts drive tax outcomes; (3) LIFETIME GIFTING using annual exclusions and lifetime exemption; (4) TRUSTS (bypass, grantor retained annuity, generation-skipping); (5) CHARITABLE strategies (private foundations, DAFs, CRUTs); coordinate portfolio and estate to maximize wealth transfer.
new_option: portfolio construction INTEGRATES with estate planning: (1) STEP-UP in basis on death for taxable assets — hold most-appreciated assets in taxable for step-up.

original_option: TAX-EFFICIENT WITHDRAWAL SEQUENCE typically: (1) TAXABLE accounts first (already-taxed income; harvest gains at lower LT capital gains rate; step-up planning); (2) TAX-DEFERRED (401k/IRA) next (avoid RMDs pushing income into higher brackets); (3) ROTH LAST (grow tax-free longest); actual sequence depends on tax bracket management, RMDs, healthcare planning; some clients benefit from ROTH CONVERSIONS in low-income years.
new_option: TAX-EFFICIENT WITHDRAWAL SEQUENCE typically: (1) TAXABLE accounts first (already-taxed income.

original_option: 4.44% — TEY = 2.8% / (1 − 0.37) = 2.8% / 0.63 = 4.44%; investor should compare 2.8% muni yield to any TAXABLE bond yielding LESS THAN 4.44%; if Treasury yields 4.10%, the muni provides higher after-tax return.

if portfolio generates 30% of return from dividends (35% rate), 60% from long-term gains (20% rate), and 10% from short-term gains (37% rate), BLENDED effective rate = 0.30 × 0.35 + 0.60 × 0.20 + 0.10 × 0.37 = 0.105 + 0.120 + 0.037 = 0.262 or 26.2%; after-tax return = pre-tax × (1 − 0.262) = pre-tax × 0.738.
new_option: if portfolio generates 30% of return from dividends (35% rate), 60% from long-term gains (20% rate), and 10% from short-term gains (37% rate), BLENDED effective rate = 0.30 × 0.35 + 0.60 × 0.20 + 0.10 × 0.37 = 0.105 + 0.120 + 0.037 = 0.262 or 26.2%.

original_option: GOALS-BASED reporting shows: (1) each GOAL's portfolio value vs its target; (2) PROBABILITY of achieving each goal given current position; (3) RETURN vs each goal-specific BENCHMARK; (4) shortfall analysis if underfunded; (5) client can see which goals are on track vs at risk; enables PRIORITIZATION and clear tradeoffs; aggregate view supplements but doesn't replace goal-level view.
new_option: GOALS-BASED reporting shows: (1) each GOAL's portfolio value vs its target.

original_option: 3.57% — (1.072 / 1.035) − 1 = 1.0357 − 1 = 3.57%; approximation 7.2% − 3.5% = 3.7% overstates the true real return; the exact Fisher approach is more accurate especially at higher inflation.

when rebalancing, PREFER trades in TAX-DEFERRED accounts (no capital gains tax); if must trade in TAXABLE, HARVEST tax losses to offset gains; USE NEW cash flows (savings, dividends) to buy underweight assets rather than SELL overweight ones; consider TAX-EFFICIENT vehicles (ETFs vs mutual funds, direct index vs commingled) to reduce forced distributions.
new_option: when rebalancing, PREFER trades in TAX-DEFERRED accounts (no capital gains tax).

original_option: TAX-EFFICIENT CHARITABLE STRATEGIES: (1) GIVE APPRECIATED SECURITIES held long-term (avoid capital gains tax, deduct FMV); (2) DONOR-ADVISED FUNDS (bunched contribution years for higher itemized deduction, then grant over time); (3) QUALIFIED CHARITABLE DISTRIBUTIONS from IRAs (age 70½+, up to annual limit); (4) CHARITABLE REMAINDER TRUSTS (income to client + eventual gift to charity); (5) PRIVATE FOUNDATIONS for large-scale giving; strategy depends on scale and goals.
new_option: TAX-EFFICIENT CHARITABLE STRATEGIES: (1) GIVE APPRECIATED SECURITIES held long-term (avoid capital gains tax, deduct FMV).

original_option: ROTH CONVERSION converts pre-tax IRA to Roth (tax on conversion but future tax-free growth); FAVORABLE when: (1) current tax rate LOWER than expected future rate; (2) low-income years (early retirement before RMDs, sabbatical); (3) able to pay conversion tax from TAXABLE assets (not from the IRA); (4) long horizon to compound tax-free; UNFAVORABLE when opposite; requires multi-year tax planning integrated with income and RMD projections.
new_option: ROTH CONVERSION converts pre-tax IRA to Roth (tax on conversion but future tax-free growth).
